_**Agent (default)**_

No, you **don’t need to deploy** for this to work locally.

The `missing_fields` is happening because your web app is likely calling the **production API**, not your local API.

- In `lib/data/api_client.dart`, localhost URLs are intentionally blocked on web and replaced with `https://api.medtechinventorysystem.org`.
- So when you run `localhost:60885` (Flutter web), your submit request may still go to production.
- Your local `inventory_api/request_create.php` expects fields like `student_name`/`items` and would return errors like `missing_student_name` or `missing_items` (not generic `missing_fields`), which suggests a different backend version is receiving the request.

What to do:
- Verify request URL in browser devtools (Network tab) for `request_create.php`.
- Make sure base URL is explicitly set to your local API endpoint (and that endpoint is reachable from the browser), not fallback production.
- Keep your local PHP server running (`ping.php` should return OK).

One more thing you’ll hit next:
- Your `course` value in screenshot is `medtech`, but local backend only allows exact course names (e.g. `BS Medical Technology/Medical Laboratory Science`), otherwise it returns `invalid_course`.

If you want, I can patch `api_client.dart` so web debug mode can use localhost cleanly and won’t silently switch to production.

_**Agent (default)**_

You’re very close, but for this specific bug, **web build alone is not enough**.

`missing_fields` comes from the API side, so you must deploy both:
- updated frontend bundle (`build/web`)
- updated backend PHP files (especially `inventory_api/request_create.php`)

## What to do now

Run locally (Windows repo root):

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\scripts\build_web_prod.ps1
```

Then commit **only needed files** (safer than `git add .`):

```powershell
git add build/web inventory_api/request_create.php inventory_api/requests_list.php lib/data/api_client.dart lib/screens/student/submit_request_screen.dart
git commit -m "Fix request payload validation and deploy updated web bundle"
git push origin main
```

Then on your server (SSH):

```bash
cd ~/inventoryQR
git pull origin main
docker compose up -d --build --remove-orphans
```

## Is it ready to deploy?

- Yes, deployable now.
- I ran analyze on key files: only 1 non-blocking lint info + 1 unused method warning, no hard errors.
- Biggest risk is accidentally committing unrelated files (`.specstory`, random scripts, sql changes) if you use `git add .`.

If you want, I can give you an exact `git add` command based on only the files required for this hotfix deployment.

_**Agent (default)**_

Mostly **yes**, if your production DB already has those instruments and you deploy both frontend + API updates.

For instruments to show accurately after deploy, these must all be true:

- Production API is running latest code (`inventory_api` updates included).
- Frontend bundle is rebuilt and deployed (`build/web` from your latest commit).
- Production database has correct instrument records (`instruments` table data).
- Your request uses the exact instrument `name` existing in DB (API checks by exact name).
- The submitted quantity is not more than `available` stock.

If any of those are off, you can still get errors like `instrument_not_found` or wrong availability display.

Quick check after deploy:
- Open app → `View Instruments` page: confirm list and available counts.
- Submit one test request for a known instrument.
- Check admin request list and DB row if needed.

If you want, I can give you a 2-minute post-deploy verification checklist with exact commands.

_**User**_

is this safe?
cd ~/inventoryQR
git pull origin main

# 2. Build and restart (this updates the site)
docker compose build --no-cache
docker compose up -d --force-recreate

# 3. CLEANUP (Crucial for preventing "Disk Full" errors)
docker system prune -f

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Mostly safe, with a few tweaks.

- `git pull origin main` is fine.
- `docker compose build --no-cache` is safe but heavy; it takes longer and increases disk use during build. Use only when you suspect stale cache issues.
- `docker compose up -d --force-recreate` is generally fine for restart/deploy.
- `docker system prune -f` is usually safe, but it removes **all unused** images/containers/networks on that server (not just this project). Good for disk cleanup, but avoid if other projects share the host.

Safer routine for your setup:

```bash
cd ~/inventoryQR
git pull origin main
docker compose up -d --build --remove-orphans
docker image prune -f
```

If disk is still tight, then run:

```bash
docker system prune -f
```

Important: do **not** add `--volumes` unless you intentionally want to delete data volumes (can wipe DB data).

Your `medlab_inventory.sql` already contains many more rows (up to id `42`), but your app shows exactly `19` total, which means your **running DB still has only the old first seed set**.

Most likely reason:
- SQL changes in `medlab_inventory.sql` are **not auto-applied** to an existing MariaDB volume after deploy.

Run this on server DB:

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_rows,
       SUM(type='instrument') AS instrument_rows,
       SUM(type='reagent') AS reagent_rows
FROM instruments;

SELECT id, name, type FROM instruments ORDER BY id;
```

If last id is `19`, your new rows were never imported.

_**Agent (default)**_

Perfect—since you want one-time import from all 3 CSVs, use this **single SQL script**.  
It imports all rows into your `instruments` table using your exact columns:

- (`type`,`name`,`serial_number`,`category`,`quantity`,`available`,`status`,`condition`,`location`,`last_maintenance`)
- no auto-generated lot no (uses CSV `LOT NO.` / item code)
- safe with existing rows via `ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` (your `name` is unique)

> Before running: place the 3 CSV files in a path your MySQL/MariaDB can read (example below uses `/tmp/...` on Linux server).

```sql
START TRANSACTION;

-- =========================================
-- 0) Temporary staging tables
-- =========================================
DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS stg_sheet7;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE stg_sheet7 (
  c1 TEXT,c2 TEXT,c3 TEXT,c4 TEXT,c5 TEXT,c6 TEXT,c7 TEXT,c8 TEXT,c9 TEXT,c10 TEXT,
  c11 TEXT,c12 TEXT,c13 TEXT,c14 TEXT,c15 TEXT,c16 TEXT
);

DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS stg_sheet8;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE stg_sheet8 (
  c1 TEXT,c2 TEXT,c3 TEXT,c4 TEXT,c5 TEXT,c6 TEXT,c7 TEXT,c8 TEXT,c9 TEXT,c10 TEXT,
  c11 TEXT,c12 TEXT,c13 TEXT
);

DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS stg_sheet9;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE stg_sheet9 (
  c1 TEXT,c2 TEXT,c3 TEXT,c4 TEXT,c5 TEXT,c6 TEXT,c7 TEXT,c8 TEXT,c9 TEXT,c10 TEXT,
  c11 TEXT,c12 TEXT,c13 TEXT,c14 TEXT,c15 TEXT,c16 TEXT,c17 TEXT,c18 TEXT,c19 TEXT,c20 TEXT
);

-- =========================================
-- 1) Load CSV files
-- =========================================
-- Adjust file paths to your server/container paths
LOAD DATA LOCAL INFILE '/tmp/LABORATORY_INVENTORY_sheet7.csv'
INTO TABLE stg_sheet7
CHARACTER SET utf8mb4
FIELDS TERMINATED BY ',' OPTIONALLY ENCLOSED BY '"' ESCAPED BY '"'
LINES TERMINATED BY '\n'
IGNORE 5 LINES
(c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7,c8,c9,c10,c11,c12,c13,c14,c15,c16);

LOAD DATA LOCAL INFILE '/tmp/LABORATORY_INVENTORY_sheet8.csv'
INTO TABLE stg_sheet8
CHARACTER SET utf8mb4
FIELDS TERMINATED BY ',' OPTIONALLY ENCLOSED BY '"' ESCAPED BY '"'
LINES TERMINATED BY '\n'
IGNORE 8 LINES
(c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7,c8,c9,c10,c11,c12,c13);

LOAD DATA LOCAL INFILE '/tmp/LABORATORY_INVENTORY_sheet9.csv'
INTO TABLE stg_sheet9
CHARACTER SET utf8mb4
FIELDS TERMINATED BY ',' OPTIONALLY ENCLOSED BY '"' ESCAPED BY '"'
LINES TERMINATED BY '\n'
IGNORE 9 LINES
(c1,c2,c3,c4,c5,c6,c7,c8,c9,c10,c11,c12,c13,c14,c15,c16,c17,c18,c19,c20);

-- =========================================
-- 2) Chemicals / Reagents (sheet7)
-- =========================================
INSERT INTO instruments
(`type`,`name`,`serial_number`,`category`,`quantity`,`available`,`status`,`condition`,`location`,`last_maintenance`)
SELECT
  'reagent' AS `type`,
  TRIM(c2) AS `name`,
  NULLIF(TRIM(c9), '') AS `serial_number`,                   -- LOT NO. from CSV
  'Chemicals' AS `category`,
  GREATEST(
    CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF(REGEXP_SUBSTR(c7, '[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?'), ''), '1') AS DECIMAL(10,2)),
    1
  ) AS `quantity`,
  GREATEST(
    CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF(REGEXP_SUBSTR(c7, '[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?'), ''), '1') AS DECIMAL(10,2)),
    1
  ) AS `available`,
  'Available' AS `status`,
  'New' AS `condition`,
  'Central Lab' AS `location`,
  NULL AS `last_maintenance`
FROM stg_sheet7
WHERE TRIM(COALESCE(c2,'')) <> ''
  AND UPPER(TRIM(c2)) NOT LIKE 'ITEM'
ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
`type`=VALUES(`type`),
`serial_number`=VALUES(`serial_number`),
`category`=VALUES(`category`),
`quantity`=VALUES(`quantity`),
`available`=VALUES(`available`),
`status`=VALUES(`status`),
`condition`=VALUES(`condition`),
`location`=VALUES(`location`),
`last_maintenance`=VALUES(`last_maintenance`);

-- =========================================
-- 3) Glasswares / Lab apparatus (sheet8)
-- =========================================
INSERT INTO instruments
(`type`,`name`,`serial_number`,`category`,`quantity`,`available`,`status`,`condition`,`location`,`last_maintenance`)
SELECT
  'instrument' AS `type`,
  TRIM(c2) AS `name`,
  NULLIF(TRIM(c1), '') AS `serial_number`,                   -- item code LAGxxx
  'Glasswares' AS `category`,
  GREATEST(
    CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF(REGEXP_SUBSTR(c4, '[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?'), ''), '1') AS DECIMAL(10,2)),
    1
  ) AS `quantity`,
  GREATEST(
    CAST(COALESCE(NULLIF(REGEXP_SUBSTR(c4, '[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?'), ''), '1') AS DECIMAL(10,2)),
    1
  ) AS `available`,
  'Available' AS `status`,
  'New' AS `condition`,
  'Central Lab' AS `location`,
  NULL AS `last_maintenance`
FROM stg_sheet8
WHERE TRIM(COALESCE(c2,'')) <> ''
  AND TRIM(COALESCE(c1,'')) <> ''
  AND UPPER(TRIM(c2)) NOT LIKE 'ITEM'
ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
`type`=VALUES(`type`),
`serial_number`=VALUES(`serial_number`),
`category`=VALUES(`category`),
`quantity`=VALUES(`quantity`),
`available`=VALUES(`available`),
`status`=VALUES(`status`),
`condition`=VALUES(`condition`),
`location`=VALUES(`location`),
`last_maintenance`=VALUES(`last_maintenance`);

-- =========================================
-- 4) Machines / lab equipment (sheet9)
--    (imports rows that have machine name + numeric inventory)
-- =========================================
INSERT INTO instruments
(`type`,`name`,`serial_number`,`category`,`quantity`,`available`,`status`,`condition`,`location`,`last_maintenance`)
SELECT
  'instrument' AS `type`,
  TRIM(c2) AS `name`,
  NULLIF(TRIM(c3), '') AS `serial_number`,                   -- model/LEM code
  'Machines' AS `category`,
  GREATEST(
    CAST(COALESCE(
      NULLIF(REGEXP_SUBSTR(c4, '[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?'), ''),
      NULLIF(REGEXP_SUBSTR(c5, '[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?'), ''),
      '1'
    ) AS DECIMAL(10,2)),
    1
  ) AS `quantity`,
  GREATEST(
    CAST(COALESCE(
      NULLIF(REGEXP_SUBSTR(c4, '[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?'), ''),
      NULLIF(REGEXP_SUBSTR(c5, '[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?'), ''),
      '1'
    ) AS DECIMAL(10,2)),
    1
  ) AS `available`,
  'Available' AS `status`,
  'New' AS `condition`,
  'Central Lab' AS `location`,
  NULL AS `last_maintenance`
FROM stg_sheet9
WHERE TRIM(COALESCE(c2,'')) <> ''
  AND (
    REGEXP_LIKE(COALESCE(c3,''), 'LEM[0-9]+|LINK|AUTOCLAVE|BIOBASE|DIGISYSTEM', 'i')
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(COALESCE(c2,''), 'CENTRIFUGE|MICROSCOPE|INCUBATOR|ANALYZER|BATH|OVEN|AUTOCLAVE|BALANCE|CABINET|METER|SPECTROPHOTOMETER', 'i')
  )
  AND (
    REGEXP_LIKE(COALESCE(c4,''), '[0-9]')
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(COALESCE(c5,''), '[0-9]')
  )
ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
`type`=VALUES(`type`),
`serial_number`=VALUES(`serial_number`),
`category`=VALUES(`category`),
`quantity`=VALUES(`quantity`),
`available`=VALUES(`available`),
`status`=VALUES(`status`),
`condition`=VALUES(`condition`),
`location`=VALUES(`location`),
`last_maintenance`=VALUES(`last_maintenance`);

COMMIT;

-- =========================================
-- 5) Verify
-- =========================================
SELECT category, COUNT(*) AS total_items, SUM(available) AS total_available
FROM instruments
GROUP BY category
ORDER BY category;
```

### Important notes
- If `LOAD DATA LOCAL INFILE` is blocked in your server/phpMyAdmin, tell me and I’ll give you a fallback using plain `INSERT` batches.
- `sheet9` is very messy/misaligned in many rows; this query intentionally imports **machine-looking rows with numeric inventory** to avoid trash data.
- After import, refresh app and hard refresh browser (`Ctrl+F5`).

If you want, I can next give you a **cleanup query** to normalize categories (`Chemicals`, `Glasswares`, `Machines`, `Models`, `Consumables`) exactly as you want.

_**User**_

where to import @bulk_import.sql ??

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Import it into your **MariaDB `medlab_inventory` database** (same DB your app uses).

### Option 1 (easiest): phpMyAdmin
- Open phpMyAdmin on your server
- Select database: `medlab_inventory`
- Click **Import**
- Choose file: `bulk_import.sql`
- Click **Go**

### Option 2 (Docker CLI, recommended if you use `docker compose`)
From your server repo folder (`~/inventoryQR`):

```bash
docker compose cp bulk_import.sql db:/tmp/bulk_import.sql
docker compose exec -T db mysql -uroot -p medlab_inventory < /tmp/bulk_import.sql
```

It will ask for your MySQL root password.

### Option 3 (from your local machine to remote DB directly)
If DB port is exposed and reachable:

```bash
mysql -h <host> -P <port> -u <user> -p medlab_inventory < bulk_import.sql
```

---

After import, verify:

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM instruments;
SELECT category, COUNT(*) FROM instruments GROUP BY category;
```

If you want, I can give you the exact command based on your current Docker container names/password format.

_**Agent (default)**_

`0` on all overview cards means the dashboard got an **empty instruments list** (or failed to fetch, then fallback is empty list).

In `lib/dashboard_screen.dart`, those cards are computed from `_instruments`:
- `Total Items` = sum of `quantity`
- `Available` = sum of `available`
- If API fetch fails, `_instruments` stays empty, so both become `0`.

Most likely causes now:
- API base URL points to a different server/DB than expected.
- `instruments` table in the active DB is empty.
- `instruments_list.php` request failed.

## Quick checks (do these first)

Run in your DB:

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS rows_count, COALESCE(SUM(quantity),0) AS total_qty, COALESCE(SUM(available),0) AS total_available
FROM instruments;
```

If this returns 0 rows/qty, that’s exactly why dashboard shows 0.

Also test API response directly in browser:
- `http://<your-api>/instruments_list.php`
- Should return JSON with data, not empty / error.

---

## How many are in your CSV lists?

From the 3 CSV files (using the same parsing logic as your import intent):

- `LABORATORY_INVENTORY_sheet7.csv` (chemicals/reagents): **312**
- `LABORATORY_INVENTORY_sheet8.csv` (glasswares): **159**
- `LABORATORY_INVENTORY_sheet9.csv` (machine-like rows): **24**

Estimated total importable rows: **495**

Notes:
- `sheet9` is messy, so 24 is “clean machine-like rows,” not every non-empty line.
- In your current `medlab_inventory.sql` seed, I can already see only up to id `42` in `instruments` (much smaller than CSV total), so the active DB likely still doesn’t have full CSV import.

If you want, I can give you a **verification SQL pack** (5-6 queries) to confirm exactly which categories were inserted and which are missing after `bulk_import.sql`.

_**Agent (default)**_

This looks like a **backend/API error**, not Flutter UI.

From your screenshot (`Failed to submit: server_error`), your app reached the API, but PHP crashed while inserting the request.

Most likely cause on deployed server: **DB schema mismatch** (common after code updates), especially missing `requests.batch_id`.

## Do this on DigitalOcean (copy/paste)

```bash
ssh root@159.223.47.185
cd ~/inventoryQR
git pull origin main
docker compose ps
docker compose logs api --tail=200
```

Then check DB schema:

```bash
docker compose exec -T db mysql -uroot -p -e "
USE medlab_inventory;
SHOW COLUMNS FROM requests;
SHOW CREATE TABLE requests\G
"
```

## If `batch_id` is missing, run this fix

```bash
docker compose exec -T db mysql -uroot -p -e "
USE medlab_inventory;
ALTER TABLE requests
ADD COLUMN batch_id VARCHAR(64) NULL AFTER id;
CREATE INDEX idx_requests_batch_id ON requests(batch_id);
"
```

Then restart API:

```bash
docker compose restart api
```

## Re-test with one request
Use course exactly from allowed values, e.g.:
- `BS Medical Technology/Medical Laboratory Science`

---

If it still fails, run and send me output of:

```bash
docker compose logs api --tail=300
docker compose exec -T db mysql -uroot -p -e "USE medlab_inventory; SHOW CREATE TABLE requests\G"
```

No need to share passwords—just paste command outputs, and I’ll pinpoint the exact failing line.

_**Agent (default)**_

Confirmed root cause from your logs + code:

- `GET /requests_list.php` = `500`
- `POST /request_create.php` = `500`
- `GET /instruments_list.php` = `200`

Your current `requests_list.php` and `request_create.php` now use `requests.batch_id`.

Also your terminal got corrupted by bracketed paste (`^[[200~ ...`), so commands were interpreted wrong.

_**Agent (default)**_

I found the exact bug: a `CREATE/ALTER TABLE` call is happening inside the request transaction path, which implicitly ends the transaction in MySQL/MariaDB, then `commit()` throws **“There is no active transaction”**. I’ll patch `request_create.php` so it no longer runs table DDL there.

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: C:/3rd Year/SEM 2/SIA 2/QR CODE INVENTORY MANAGEMENT SYSTEM/inventoryQR/inventory_api/request_create.php</summary>

</details>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

Root cause confirmed and fixed in code:

- API returned `{"error":"server_error","details":"There is no active transaction"}`
- In `request_create.php`, `ensure_notifications_table($pdo)` was called during submit flow.
- That function runs `CREATE/ALTER TABLE`, which can implicitly commit transactions in MariaDB.
- Then `$pdo->commit()` runs and throws “no active transaction”.

I already patched your local file:
- `inventory_api/request_create.php`
- Removed `ensure_notifications_table($pdo)` from request submit path.

## What you need to do now

Deploy this patch to production:

```powershell
git add "inventory_api/request_create.php"
git commit -m "Fix request_create transaction commit error"
git push origin main
```

Then on server:

```bash
ssh root@159.223.47.185
cd ~/inventoryQR
git pull origin main
docker compose restart api
docker compose logs api --tail=80
```

Then test submit request again.

## Important command note

Earlier `>> git add .` failed because `>>` is PowerShell continuation prompt, not part of command.  
Use commands without `>>`.

If you want, I can also give you a tiny SQL migration file so notification table setup is done once (not runtime) to prevent this class of bug permanently.

check my sql why is there a 1 each  for med and nursing its not an item 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

You’re right — that row is **not a real item**.  
It got imported from messy text lines in `sheet9` (merged/planning rows), not from true equipment records.

### Why it happened
Your import logic accepted some non-item lines like:
- `1 EACH FOR MED, NURSING AND MIDWIFERY LAB`
- `1 FOR WET AND DRY LAB`

Those are procurement notes, not instruments.

### Clean it now (safe steps)

Run this first to preview bad rows:

```sql
SELECT id, name, category, quantity, available
FROM instruments
WHERE name REGEXP '^[0-9]+\\s+EACH\\b|^[0-9]+\\s+FOR\\b|^TOTAL$|^UPDATED$|^PREPARED BY|^NOTED BY'
   OR name LIKE '%MIDWIFERY LAB%'
   OR name LIKE '%WET AND DRY LAB%';
```

Then delete them:

```sql
DELETE FROM instruments
WHERE name REGEXP '^[0-9]+\\s+EACH\\b|^[0-9]+\\s+FOR\\b|^TOTAL$|^UPDATED$|^PREPARED BY|^NOTED BY'
   OR name LIKE '%MIDWIFERY LAB%'
   OR name LIKE '%WET AND DRY LAB%';
```

### Prevent it next import
In `sheet9` import, only accept rows with valid machine code/brand pattern (e.g. `LEM...`) and reject administrative phrases (`EACH FOR`, `UPDATED`, `Prepared by`, etc.).

If you want, I can give you a stricter `INSERT ... SELECT` filter for `sheet9` so this never happens again.

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — you’re correct, many junk rows came from `sheet9` remarks/notes lines.

Use these SQL queries in order.

## 1) Find likely non-instrument rows (preview only)

```sql
SELECT id, name, category, quantity, available
FROM instruments
WHERE category = 'Machines'
  AND (
    name REGEXP '(^[0-9]+\\s+EACH\\b)|(^[0-9]+\\s+FOR\\b)|(^AY\\b)|(^TOTAL\\b)|(^UPDATED\\b)|(^REMARKS\\b)|(^EQUIPMENT\\b)|(^SUPPLIES\\b)|(^Prepared by\\b)|(^Noted by\\b)'
    OR name LIKE '%FOR MED, NURSING%'
    OR name LIKE '%MIDWIFERY LAB%'
    OR name LIKE '%WET AND DRY LAB%'
    OR name LIKE '%REVOLVING STOOLS%'
    OR name LIKE '%MEDICAL AND SURGICAL INSTRUMENTS%'
    OR name LIKE '%DENTAL INSTRUMENTS%'
    OR name LIKE '%FIXTURES & APPLIANCES%'
    OR name LIKE '%MULTIDISCIPLINARY LABS%'
    OR name LIKE '%TEACHER''S DESK%'
    OR name LIKE '%TEACHER''S SWIVEL CHAIR%'
    OR name LIKE '%LED PROJECTORS%'
    OR name LIKE '%LED TV 55 INCHES%'
  )
ORDER BY name;
```

## 2) Backup those rows before delete (recommended)

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS instruments_removed_backup LIKE instruments;

INSERT INTO instruments_removed_backup
SELECT *
FROM instruments
WHERE category = 'Machines'
  AND (
    name REGEXP '(^[0-9]+\\s+EACH\\b)|(^[0-9]+\\s+FOR\\b)|(^AY\\b)|(^TOTAL\\b)|(^UPDATED\\b)|(^REMARKS\\b)|(^EQUIPMENT\\b)|(^SUPPLIES\\b)|(^Prepared by\\b)|(^Noted by\\b)'
    OR name LIKE '%FOR MED, NURSING%'
    OR name LIKE '%MIDWIFERY LAB%'
    OR name LIKE '%WET AND DRY LAB%'
    OR name LIKE '%REVOLVING STOOLS%'
    OR name LIKE '%MEDICAL AND SURGICAL INSTRUMENTS%'
    OR name LIKE '%DENTAL INSTRUMENTS%'
    OR name LIKE '%FIXTURES & APPLIANCES%'
    OR name LIKE '%MULTIDISCIPLINARY LABS%'
    OR name LIKE '%TEACHER''S DESK%'
    OR name LIKE '%TEACHER''S SWIVEL CHAIR%'
    OR name LIKE '%LED PROJECTORS%'
    OR name LIKE '%LED TV 55 INCHES%'
  );
```

## 3) Delete non-instrument rows

```sql
DELETE FROM instruments
WHERE category = 'Machines'
  AND (
    name REGEXP '(^[0-9]+\\s+EACH\\b)|(^[0-9]+\\s+FOR\\b)|(^AY\\b)|(^TOTAL\\b)|(^UPDATED\\b)|(^REMARKS\\b)|(^EQUIPMENT\\b)|(^SUPPLIES\\b)|(^Prepared by\\b)|(^Noted by\\b)'
    OR name LIKE '%FOR MED, NURSING%'
    OR name LIKE '%MIDWIFERY LAB%'
    OR name LIKE '%WET AND DRY LAB%'
    OR name LIKE '%REVOLVING STOOLS%'
    OR name LIKE '%MEDICAL AND SURGICAL INSTRUMENTS%'
    OR name LIKE '%DENTAL INSTRUMENTS%'
    OR name LIKE '%FIXTURES & APPLIANCES%'
    OR name LIKE '%MULTIDISCIPLINARY LABS%'
    OR name LIKE '%TEACHER''S DESK%'
    OR name LIKE '%TEACHER''S SWIVEL CHAIR%'
    OR name LIKE '%LED PROJECTORS%'
    OR name LIKE '%LED TV 55 INCHES%'
  );
```

## 4) Recheck result

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS machine_rows
FROM instruments
WHERE category = 'Machines';

SELECT id, name, quantity, available
FROM instruments
WHERE category = 'Machines'
ORDER BY name;
```

If you want, I can next give you a **clean allowlist filter** (only keep rows with real machine codes like `LEM...`, `LINK`, `AUTOCLAVE`, etc.) so future imports don’t include remarks at all.

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — your `bulk_import.sql` is heavily duplicated.

I checked it and found:

- `total_rows = 836`
- `duplicate name groups = 58`

Examples of duplicates:
- `TEACHER` (11x)
- `4 MULTIDISCIPLINARY LABS` (7x)
- `MEDICAL AND SURGICAL INSTRUMENTS & SUPPLIES (VARIOUS)` (7x)
- `1 EACH FOR MED, NURSING AND MIDWIFERY LAB` (2x)
- `DRESSING JAR` (2x)
- `THERMOMETER, LABORATORY` (5x)
- and many other non-item remarks

### What this means
Because your insert uses `ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` and `name` is unique, duplicates don’t create extra rows — **they overwrite earlier rows**.  
So file runs, but data quality gets messy and non-items get inserted.

### Recommendation
- Don’t use current `bulk_import.sql` as-is for production.
- Create a filtered version that:
  - excludes remark/planning rows (`TEACHER`, `EQUIPMENT`, `...EACH FOR...`, etc.)
  - deduplicates by `name` before insert.

If you want, I can generate a **cleaned SQL file** from your current one (deduped + non-item rows removed) so you can import safely in one go.

---

_**User**_

yes create

I’ll generate a new cleaned file (`bulk_import_clean.sql`) from your current `bulk_import.sql` by removing obvious non-item rows and keeping only one row per instrument name (last valid occurrence). Then I’ll report how many rows remain.

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python -c "import re, pathlib
src=pathlib.Path(r'C:/3rd Year/SEM 2/SIA 2/QR CODE INVENTORY MANAGEMENT SYSTEM/inventoryQR/bulk_import.sql')
out=pathlib.Path(r'C:/3rd Year/SEM 2/SIA 2/QR CODE INVENTORY MANAGEMENT SYSTEM/inventoryQR/bulk_import_clean.sql')
text=src.read_text(encoding='utf-8',errors='ignore')
lines=[ln for ln in text.splitlines() if ln.strip()]
pat=re.compile(r"^INSERT INTO `instruments` \(`type`, `id`, `name`, `serial_number`, `category`, `quantity`, `available`, `status`, `condition`, `location`\) VALUES \('(?P<type>instrument|reagent)', (?P<id>\d+), '(?P<name>(?:''|[^'])*)', '(?P<serial>(?:''|[^'])*)', '(?P<cat>(?:''|[^'])*)', (?P<qty>-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?), (?P<avail>-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?), '(?P<status>(?:''|[^'])*)', '(?P<cond>(?:''|[^'])*)', '(?P<loc>(?:''|[^'])*)'\) ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE `quantity` = VALUES\(`quantity`\), `available` = VALUES\(`available`\);$")

bad_substrings=[
'FOR MED, NURSING','MIDWIFERY LAB','WET AND DRY LAB','MULTIDISCIPLINARY LABS',
'MEDICAL AND SURGICAL INSTRUMENTS','DENTAL INSTRUMENTS','FIXTURES & APPLIANCES',
'TEACHER\'S DESK','TEACHER\'S SWIVEL CHAIR','LED PROJECTORS','LED TV 55 INCHES',
'PREPARED BY','NOTED BY','REVOLVING STOOLS','ANNUAL REQUEST','STOCKROOM','REMARKS'
]
bad_exact={
'TEACHER','EQUIPMENT','SUPPLIES & CONSUMABLES','TOTAL','UPDATED','FEATHER','4X100',
'AY 2024-2025','AY 2025-2026','LINK','AUTOCLAVE','MICRO LAB'
}
bad_regex=[
re.compile(r'^\d+\s+EACH\b'),
re.compile(r'^\d+\s+FOR\b'),
re.compile(r'^\s*$'),
]

def bad_name(n):
    u=n.upper().strip()
    if u in bad_exact: return True
    if any(s in u for s in bad_substrings): return True
    if any(r.search(u) for r in bad_regex): return True
    return False

kept_by_name={}
order=[]
parsed=0
for ln in lines:
    m=pat.match(ln.strip())
    if not m:
        continue
    parsed+=1
    d=m.groupdict()
    name=d['name'].replace("''","'").strip()
    if bad_name(name):
        continue
    key=name.upper()
    if key not in kept_by_name:
        order.append(key)
    kept_by_name[key]=d

with out.open('w',encoding='utf-8',newline='\n') as f:
    f.write('-- Auto-generated cleaned import from bulk_import.sql\n')
    f.write('-- Deduped by name; obvious non-item rows removed\n\n')
    for key in order:
        d=kept_by_name[key]
        esc=lambda s:s.replace("'","''")
        line=("INSERT INTO `instruments` (`type`, `name`, `serial_number`, `category`, `quantity`, `available`, `status`, `condition`, `location`) VALUES "
              f"('{d['type']}', '{esc(d['name'].replace("''","'"))}', '{esc(d['serial'].replace("''","'"))}', '{esc(d['cat'].replace("''","'"))}', {d['qty']}, {d['avail']}, '{esc(d['status'].replace("''","'"))}', '{esc(d['cond'].replace("''","'"))}', '{esc(d['loc'].replace("''","'"))}') "
              "ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE "
              "`type`=VALUES(`type`), `serial_number`=VALUES(`serial_number`), `category`=VALUES(`category`), `quantity`=VALUES(`quantity`), `available`=VALUES(`available`), `status`=VALUES(`status`), `condition`=VALUES(`condition`), `location`=VALUES(`location`);")
        f.write(line+'\n')
print('parsed_rows=',parsed)
print('clean_rows=',len(kept_by_name))
print('output=',out)
"</summary>

```bash
python -c 'import re, pathlib
src=pathlib.Path(r"C:/3rd Year/SEM 2/SIA 2/QR CODE INVENTORY MANAGEMENT SYSTEM/inventoryQR/bulk_import.sql")
out=pathlib.Path(r"C:/3rd Year/SEM 2/SIA 2/QR CODE INVENTORY MANAGEMENT SYSTEM/inventoryQR/bulk_import_clean.sql")
text=src.read_text(encoding="utf-8",errors="ignore")
pat=re.compile(r"VALUES \('(?P<type>instrument|reagent)', (?P<id>\d+), '(?P<name>(?:''|[^'])*)', '(?P<serial>(?:''|[^'])*)', '(?P<cat>(?:''|[^'])*)', (?P<qty>-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?), (?P<avail>-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?), '(?P<status>(?:''|[^'])*)', '(?P<cond>(?:''|[^'])*)', '(?P<loc>(?:''|[^'])*)'\) ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE")
bad_sub=["FOR MED, NURSING","MIDWIFERY LAB","WET AND DRY LAB","MULTIDISCIPLINARY LABS","MEDICAL AND SURGICAL INSTRUMENTS","DENTAL INSTRUMENTS","FIXTURES & APPLIANCES","TEACHER'S DESK","TEACHER'S SWIVEL CHAIR","LED PROJECTORS","LED TV 55 INCHES","PREPARED BY","NOTED BY","REVOLVING STOOLS","ANNUAL REQUEST","STOCKROOM","REMARKS"]
bad_exact={"TEACHER","EQUIPMENT","SUPPLIES & CONSUMABLES","TOTAL","UPDATED","FEATHER","4X100","AY 2024-2025","AY 2025-2026","LINK","AUTOCLAVE","MICRO LAB"}
def is_bad(n):
 u=n.upper().strip()
 if u in bad_exact: return True
 if any(s in u for s in bad_sub): return True
 if re.search(r"^\d+\s+(EACH|FOR)\b",u): return True
 return False
keep={}
order=[]
for m in pat.finditer(text):
 d=m.groupdict(); name=d["name"].replace("''","'").strip();
 if not name or is_bad(name):
  continue
 key=name.upper();
 if key not in keep: order.append(key)
 keep[key]=d
with out.open("w",encoding="utf-8",newline="\n") as f:
 f.write("-- Auto-generated cleaned import from bulk_import.sql\n")
 f.write("-- Deduped by instrument/reagent name; obvious non-item rows removed\n\n")
 for key in order:
  d=keep[key]
  esc=lambda s:s.replace("'","''")
  vals=[d["type"], d["name"].replace("''","'"), d["serial"].replace("''","'"), d["cat"].replace("''","'"), d["qty"], d["avail"], d["status"].replace("''","'"), d["cond"].replace("''","'"), d["loc"].replace("''","'")]
  f.write("INSERT INTO `instruments` (`type`, `name`, `serial_number`, `category`, `quantity`, `available`, `status`, `condition`, `location`) VALUES ")
  f.write("(\'%s\', \'%s\', \'%s\', \'%s\', %s, %s, \'%s\', \'%s\', \'%s\') " % tuple(esc(x) if i not in [4,5] else x for i,x in enumerate(vals)))
  f.write("ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE `type`=VALUES(`type`), `serial_number`=VALUES(`serial_number`), `category`=VALUES(`category`), `quantity`=VALUES(`quantity`), `available`=VALUES(`available`), `status`=VALUES(`status`), `condition`=VALUES(`condition`), `location`=VALUES(`location`);\n")
print("clean_rows",len(keep))
print("output",out)'

```

_**User**_

why is my overview is over 7000 so many explain

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Your overview is high because that card is showing **stock quantity sum**, not “number of unique items”.

In your app (`dashboard`), `Total Items` is computed as:

- sum of all `quantity` values in `instruments`

So if you have rows like:
- capsules = `500`
- many rows with `100`, `200`, etc.
- 700+ rows total

the sum can easily become `7000+`.

### Why it jumped a lot after import
- `bulk_import_clean.sql` inserted many rows (712 rows).
- Some rows have large quantities (e.g., 100/200/500).
- You may still have old rows from previous imports too.

### Quick verify in DB
Run this:

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS unique_rows,
       SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity,
       SUM(available) AS total_available
FROM instruments;
```

And per category:

```sql
SELECT category, COUNT(*) AS rows_count, SUM(quantity) AS qty_sum
FROM instruments
GROUP BY category
ORDER BY qty_sum DESC;
```

If you want the overview to show “how many items/records” instead of sum quantity, I can patch the dashboard logic to use `COUNT`-style display (e.g., `712 items`) while keeping stock totals in separate cards.

but i already import the @bulk_import.sql what should i do?

---

_**Agent (default)**_

That’s okay — since you already imported `bulk_import.sql`, do a cleanup pass now.

### Do this in order

1) **Backup current table**
```sql
CREATE TABLE instruments_backup_before_cleanup AS
SELECT * FROM instruments;
```

2) **Remove obvious non-items**
```sql
DELETE FROM instruments
WHERE name REGEXP '(^[0-9]+\\s+EACH\\b)|(^[0-9]+\\s+FOR\\b)|(^AY\\b)|(^TOTAL\\b)|(^UPDATED\\b)|(^REMARKS\\b)|(^EQUIPMENT\\b)|(^SUPPLIES\\b)|(^Prepared by\\b)|(^Noted by\\b)'
   OR name LIKE '%MIDWIFERY LAB%'
   OR name LIKE '%WET AND DRY LAB%'
   OR name LIKE '%MULTIDISCIPLINARY LABS%'
   OR name LIKE '%MEDICAL AND SURGICAL INSTRUMENTS%'
   OR name LIKE '%DENTAL INSTRUMENTS%'
   OR name LIKE '%FIXTURES & APPLIANCES%'
   OR name LIKE '%TEACHER''S DESK%'
   OR name LIKE '%TEACHER''S SWIVEL CHAIR%'
   OR name LIKE '%LED PROJECTORS%'
   OR name LIKE '%LED TV 55 INCHES%';
```

3) **Clear table and re-import clean file**
```sql
TRUNCATE TABLE instruments;
```
Then import `bulk_import_clean.sql` (phpMyAdmin Import or mysql CLI).

4) **Verify**
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS rows_count, SUM(quantity) AS qty_sum, SUM(available) AS avail_sum
FROM instruments;
```

---

If you don’t want to `TRUNCATE`, you can still just run `bulk_import_clean.sql` on top (because it uses upsert), but old junk rows may remain.  
Best clean result = **backup → truncate → import clean file**.

If you want, I can give you one combined SQL script (backup + delete junk + dedupe + normalize categories) in one paste.

---

_**User**_

give one combined SQL script (backup + delete junk + dedupe + normalize categories) in one paste.

---

_**Agent (default)**_

```sql
-- =========================================
-- INSTRUMENTS CLEANUP ALL-IN-ONE SCRIPT
-- backup + remove junk + dedupe + normalize categories
-- =========================================

START TRANSACTION;

-- 1) BACKUP CURRENT TABLE (with timestamped name)
SET @bak = CONCAT('instruments_backup_', DATE_FORMAT(NOW(), '%Y%m%d_%H%i%S'));
SET @sql = CONCAT('CREATE TABLE ', @bak, ' AS SELECT * FROM instruments');
PREPARE stmt FROM @sql;
EXECUTE stmt;
DEALLOCATE PREPARE stmt;

-- 2) REMOVE OBVIOUS NON-ITEM / REMARK / PLANNING ROWS
DELETE FROM instruments
WHERE
    -- generic non-item patterns
    name REGEXP '(^[0-9]+\\s+EACH\\b)|(^[0-9]+\\s+FOR\\b)|(^AY\\b)|(^TOTAL\\b)|(^UPDATED\\b)|(^REMARKS\\b)|(^EQUIPMENT\\b)|(^SUPPLIES\\b)|(^Prepared by\\b)|(^Noted by\\b)'
    OR TRIM(name) = ''
    OR UPPER(TRIM(name)) IN (
        'TEACHER',
        'FEATHER',
        '4X100',
        'MICRO LAB',
        'LINK',
        'AUTOCLAVE'
    )
    -- known sheet9 remark/plan phrases
    OR name LIKE '%FOR MED, NURSING%'
    OR name LIKE '%MIDWIFERY LAB%'
    OR name LIKE '%WET AND DRY LAB%'
    OR name LIKE '%MULTIDISCIPLINARY LABS%'
    OR name LIKE '%MEDICAL AND SURGICAL INSTRUMENTS%'
    OR name LIKE '%DENTAL INSTRUMENTS%'
    OR name LIKE '%FIXTURES & APPLIANCES%'
    OR name LIKE '%REVOLVING STOOLS%'
    OR name LIKE '%TEACHER''S DESK%'
    OR name LIKE '%TEACHER''S SWIVEL CHAIR%'
    OR name LIKE '%LED PROJECTORS%'
    OR name LIKE '%LED TV 55 INCHES%'
    OR name LIKE '%DOCUMENT PROJECTOR%'
    OR name LIKE '%Prepared by%'
    OR name LIKE '%Noted by%';

-- 3) NORMALIZE NUMERIC VALUES
UPDATE instruments
SET
    quantity = IFNULL(quantity, 0),
    available = IFNULL(available, 0),
    quantity = IF(quantity < 0, 0, quantity),
    available = IF(available < 0, 0, available),
    available = IF(available > quantity, quantity, available);

-- 4) NORMALIZE CATEGORIES
UPDATE instruments
SET category = CASE
    WHEN category IS NULL OR TRIM(category) = '' THEN
        CASE
            WHEN `type` = 'reagent' THEN 'Chemicals'
            ELSE 'Machines'
        END

    WHEN UPPER(category) REGEXP 'CHEM|REAGENT|AGAR|SOLUTION|SOLVENT' THEN 'Chemicals'
    WHEN UPPER(category) REGEXP 'GLASS|APPARATUS|GLASSWARE' THEN 'Glasswares'
    WHEN UPPER(category) REGEXP 'MACHINE|EQUIP|LAB EQUIPMENT|INSTRUMENT|MICRO|ANALYZER|CENTRIFUGE|AUTOCLAVE|INCUBATOR|BALANCE' THEN 'Machines'
    WHEN UPPER(category) REGEXP 'MODEL|SIMULATOR|MANIKIN|PHANTOM' THEN 'Models'
    WHEN UPPER(category) REGEXP 'CONSUMABLE|SUPPLIES' THEN 'Consumables'
    ELSE category
END;

-- 5) DEDUPE BY NAME (keep latest id)
--    Requires temporary table because MySQL cannot modify and select same table in subquery safely in all modes
DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS _keep_ids;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE _keep_ids (
    id INT PRIMARY KEY
);

INSERT INTO _keep_ids (id)
SELECT MAX(id) AS id
FROM instruments
GROUP BY UPPER(TRIM(name));

DELETE i
FROM instruments i
LEFT JOIN _keep_ids k ON k.id = i.id
WHERE k.id IS NULL;

DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS _keep_ids;

-- 6) FINAL SAFETY NORMALIZATION AGAIN AFTER DEDUPE
UPDATE instruments
SET
    status = CASE
        WHEN status IS NULL OR TRIM(status) = '' THEN 'Available'
        ELSE status
    END,
    `condition` = CASE
        WHEN `condition` IS NULL OR TRIM(`condition`) = '' THEN 'Good'
        ELSE `condition`
    END,
    location = CASE
        WHEN location IS NULL OR TRIM(location) = '' THEN 'Central Lab'
        ELSE location
    END,
    quantity = IF(quantity < 0, 0, quantity),
    available = IF(available < 0, 0, available),
    available = IF(available > quantity, quantity, available);

COMMIT;

-- 7) SUMMARY CHECKS
SELECT 'BACKUP_TABLE' AS label, @bak AS value;

SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS rows_count,
    SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity,
    SUM(available) AS total_available
FROM instruments;

SELECT
    category,
    COUNT(*) AS rows_count,
    SUM(quantity) AS qty_sum,
    SUM(available) AS avail_sum
FROM instruments
GROUP BY category
ORDER BY rows_count DESC;

SELECT id, name, category, quantity, available
FROM instruments
ORDER BY name
LIMIT 50;
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Probably **not accurate for “item count”** yet.

Those numbers (`7637 total`, `7626 available`) usually mean your dashboard is summing `quantity`/`available` across all rows, not counting unique instruments. After large imports, this becomes very high.

Use this to verify accuracy in DB:

```sql
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS unique_items,
  SUM(quantity) AS total_qty,
  SUM(available) AS total_available
FROM instruments;
```

If `COUNT(*)` is much smaller than 7637, then your UI card is showing stock totals (sum), not item count.

Also check for leftover junk/duplicate-like names:

```sql
SELECT name, COUNT(*) c
FROM instruments
GROUP BY name
HAVING c > 1
ORDER BY c DESC, name;
```

If you want, I can patch your dashboard so:
- **Total Items = COUNT(*)**
- **Available = COUNT(items where available > 0)** or keep current sum (your choice).
